original_hypothesis.title: GuildVetoGovernor description construction uses raw bytes->string conversion of timelockId, potentially generating non-UTF8 strings that can break proposal tooling or create ambiguous UI display
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: GuildVetoGovernor._getVetoCalls builds a description string via `string(abi.encodePacked(timelockId))`. Since timelockId is arbitrary bytes32, this can produce non-UTF8 strings, truncated display, or confusing UI representations. While proposalId in OZ is computed from description hash, inconsistent UI display could cause governance participants to misinterpret which operation is being vetoed or fail to parse proposal metadata.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a practical governance safety concern: even if not a direct on-chain exploit, it can harm operational security by obscuring what is being vetoed. Because veto proposals are intended to be created permissionlessly, an attacker could spam confusing proposals targeting different timelockIds with non-printable bytes that appear identical in some UIs.
preconditions: Governance tooling relies on description string for human verification
Attacker can call createVeto/executeVeto with arbitrary timelockId values (permissionless for createVeto; execute requires proposal state constraints but callable)

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: bytes32 rendered as raw string, not hex

analysis_questions: Do off-chain governance tools used by the protocol properly display non-UTF8 strings?
Does proposalId depend on description text or descriptionHash (it does), and could this affect replay/uniqueness for the same timelockId?
Would changing to hex encoding be backward compatible for veto proposal creation?
validation_requirements: Test creation of veto proposals with timelockIds containing non-printable bytes and observe UI behavior
Confirm whether any monitoring/alerting parses description strings and could be bypassed

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: An attacker creates veto proposals with timelockIds that render as confusing strings, potentially causing voters to ignore legitimate veto attempts or misidentify the target operation in off-chain tooling.
prerequisites: Users rely on UI text rather than decoding calldata

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Indirect governance risk (missed veto leading to execution of harmful timelock action)
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Operational mitigation via better tooling; contract change would require governance

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No tests for description formatting / UI display safety

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Monitor veto proposal descriptions for non-hex / non-printable characters

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
